18-Wheeler Crashes in Miami Demand Immediate Legal Action — Here's Why

Miami's I-95, Palmetto Expressway, and Dolphin Expressway corridors see some of Florida's highest commercial truck traffic volumes. When a semi-truck crash occurs, victims face a legal landscape far more complex than a typical auto accident.

Federal FMCSA regulations govern trucking companies, and those carriers move quickly to dispatch their own investigators and legal teams — often within hours of a crash. Black box data, driver logs, and maintenance records can disappear or be overwritten if not formally preserved through legal action.

At WreckMatch, we've built a network of 800+ experienced law firms across the country, including Florida attorneys who handle high-stakes truck accident cases daily. We don't just refer — we match victims with the right legal representation based on the specifics of their case.

reddit_title: Hit by an 18-wheeler in Miami — what should I do first and what deadlines should I know about?
reddit_body:
If you were involved in a crash with a semi-truck or 18-wheeler in Miami, the most important thing to understand is that commercial truck accidents are legally very different from standard car accidents. Trucking companies are regulated by the FMCSA, and they often send their own investigators to the scene quickly. Your first priority should be preserving evidence — black box data, driver logs, and maintenance records can be overwritten or destroyed if you don't act fast.

Florida has specific statutes of limitations for personal injury claims, and missing those deadlines typically means losing your right to sue entirely. Beyond deadlines, liability in truck crashes can extend to the driver, the trucking company, the cargo loader, or even the vehicle manufacturer — identifying all responsible parties early is critical to maximizing your recovery.

Don't try to navigate this alone or accept early settlement offers from insurance adjusters before consulting an attorney. Sites like wreckmatch.com can match you with experienced truck accident lawyers in Florida at no cost, which is a good starting point if you're unsure where to turn. Most truck accident attorneys work on contingency, so there's no upfront cost to getting legal help.
